title: KISS bridge TX emits L2 frames without dispatch byte
description: python/src/lichen/interface/kiss/bridge.py:155-201,399. spec/02-physical-link.md §4.1: the first byte of the authenticated inner payload is a dispatch value (only 0x14=SCHC, 0x15=routing defined; constants.toml [l2.payload_dispatch]). Every KISS-originated L2 frame violates this: APRS acks b'\x06'+msg_id, rejects b'\x7f\x15'+msg_id, messages b'\x02'+..., port-1 raw host bytes verbatim. LinkLayer.send() places payload verbatim — no dispatch prepended. Worst case: host-supplied bytes beginning 0x15 0x01 are parsed by receivers as a routing Announce; 0x14... gets SCHC-decompressed. Pre-existing but in scope. Fix: allocate a dispatch value for raw/KISS application payloads in spec/02 §4.1 + constants.toml, or wrap APRS traffic in SCHC/IPv6 (spec/07 reserves mesh port 5686 for APRS-IS).
